topic: Two colleagues are both considered for the same promotion
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed, a grating soundtrack to my mounting frustration. I watched Sarah, the one who’d been circling this promotion for months, receive the congratulatory nod from Mr. Henderson. It was a slow, deliberate gesture, a public acknowledgment that felt entirely undeserved. My jaw tightened, a hard knot forming beneath my skin. I gripped the edge of my desk so tightly my knuckles bleached white. "It's just... baffling," I muttered under my breath, the sound barely audible over the keyboard clicking. I stood up abruptly, the chair scraping loudly against the linoleum floor, and walked towards the water cooler, pretending to refill my glass while my gaze remained fixed on the closed door of the executive suite.

[story 2]

"Of course, she gets it," I heard Mark say, his tone dripping with a saccharine sweetness that scraped against my nerves. I felt a flush creep up my neck, hot and undeniable. I forced a smile, a brittle, thin thing that didn't reach my eyes. Internally, a sharp, acidic feeling rose in my chest. I leaned back in my chair, deliberately letting my posture convey a studied calm, though my hands were clenched so tightly my fingernails dug crescent moons into my palms. When the discussion concluded, I didn't offer a polite handshake. Instead, I gathered my papers with exaggerated slowness, the rustle of the documents sounding unnecessarily loud in the quiet room.

[story 3]

I stared at the framed announcement on the bulletin board. Sarah’s name was there, bold and undeniable. A strange, hot pressure built behind my eyes, making the edges of the room seem to blur. I felt a sudden, intense need to dismantle the decision, to question the very metrics used to judge this advancement. I walked over to the window, pulling the blinds shut with unnecessary force, plunging my immediate workspace into shadow. "This isn't fair," I whispered, the words tasting metallic and sharp on my tongue. I turned back, my expression carefully neutral, but the tension in my shoulders was screaming a silent protest against the perceived injustice.
